what_it_does: Defines a small set of shared visual primitives every Type Registry consumer surface uses: TypeLayerBadge (coloured pip + layer label), TypeProvBadge (provenance: built-in/user/vi/imported), TypeChip (one-line clickable reference), TypeCard (full grid card with thumbnail, badges, stats, optional Use button), TypeLineageStrip (root→…→self breadcrumb), SlotAcceptsHint + TypeSlotList + FilledSlotChip (render a Type's slots and what they accept/contain), TypeFilterBar (search + segmented layer filter), and TypeTree/TypeTreeNode (nested parent→children tree grouped by layer). All read from window.CV_REGISTRY and are exported onto window.
observations: LAYER_RANK constant {token:0..template:6} is defined but not referenced within this file

TypeCard composes TypeThumb (with a plain placeholder div fallback if window.TypeThumb absent), TypeLayerBadge, TypeProvBadge, and shows stat counts: slots (◇), children (↳), variables (𝓋), plus up to 3 tags
TypeCard derives childCount from R.children(type.id) and varCount from type.variables
TypeLineageStrip reverses R.lineage(id) to render root→self with › separators
TypeTree groups query results by layer, then within each layer separates roots (no extends within the filtered set) from extending children, rendering children nested via recursive TypeTreeNode
SlotAcceptsHint formats accepts.layers/families/tags into a single hint string, defaulting to 'any Type'
FilledSlotChip resolves a typeId to a Type and shows a missing-marker chip when unresolved
TypeFilterBar reads LAYERS and LAYER_INFO swatches/labels from window.CV_REGISTRY
Badges/labels/icons all sourced from CV_REGISTRY.LAYER_INFO and type.icon with 'check-square' fallback
React hooks aliased with _tu suffix (useState_tu, useMemo_tu)
standouts: TypeTree's root-vs-child grouping is computed against the *filtered* set (roots = no extends inside the current result set), so filtering reshapes the tree
The header frames these as 'so the system feels coherent' — a deliberate shared-primitive set
useMemo deps include R?.all().length / filter to recompute on registry growth
conventions: Reads all data from window.CV_REGISTRY (interface as projection of the registry, per project rule)
Icon fallback 'check-square', class names prefixed cv-type-*
Exports the component set via Object.assign(window, {...})
No raw colors — pips/swatches come from LAYER_INFO
concerns: LAYER_RANK is declared but unused in this file (possible dead code or intended for a consumer)
Several components silently return null/empty when CV_REGISTRY or a type is absent rather than failing loud (project rule favours loud fail, though UI null-render is a common exception)
notes: The shared UI toolkit for showing Types — badges, chips, cards, lineage, slot lists, filter bar, and a recursive layer-grouped tree — all driven off CV_REGISTRY.
questions: Is LAYER_RANK intended for sorting here and left unwired, or consumed elsewhere?
What is the source/shape of CV_REGISTRY.LAYER_INFO (swatch, label, desc) these components depend on?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header promises shared visual primitives every consumer surface uses, and the file is exactly that coherent component set.
apparent_themes: Interface as a projection of the registry
Composition (cards compose thumbs+badges; trees compose nodes)
intention_signals: Trying to give every place that shows a Type (Registry, builder browser, sidebar, embedding UI) the same small vocabulary of components so the registry reads coherently everywhere and stays in sync with CV_REGISTRY by construction.
novelty: The filter-aware root/child tree reshaping and the slot-accepts hint rendering (showing what each slot will accept by layer/family/tag) are the distinctive pieces.
contribution: Contributes the reusable Type-display component library: layer/provenance badges, chip, full card, lineage strip, slot list with accept hints, search+layer filter bar, and the recursive layer-grouped type tree — the consumer-facing UI primitives for the Type registry.
summary_for_embedding: A set of shared React/browser-global UI primitives (badges, chip, card, lineage strip, slot list, filter bar, recursive tree) for displaying registry Types, all driven from window.CV_REGISTRY.
intention_for_embedding: To give every surface that shows a Type one coherent set of components so the registry UI is a projection of CV_REGISTRY and never drifts into parallel structures.
